deps.name: express
deps.reason: Core HTTP framework: routing, middleware, body handling. No native equivalent; node:http is far lower-level.

deps.name: uuid
deps.reason: Projects almost always use uuid v4 here, which crypto.randomUUID() covers and is stable from Node 16. Edge case: if v1/v3/v5/v7 were needed the native would NOT suffice, but v4 is the default usage in REST APIs.

deps.name: rimraf
deps.reason: fs.rm/fs.rmSync with recursive+force shipped in 14.14 and is stable; for plain directory cleanup it fully replaces rimraf at this floor.

deps.name: body-parser
deps.reason: Not a Node built-in but redundant: Express has bundled express.json()/express.urlencoded() (the body-parser internals) since Express 4.16, so the separate dependency is needless. Labeling as needless because the standard in-stack replacement removes the package.

deps.name: jsonwebtoken
deps.reason: Security primitive: JWT sign/verify with alg handling. node:crypto gives HMAC/RSA but not JWT encode/decode/claims validation. No native equivalent.

deps.name: morgan
deps.reason: HTTP request logging middleware with formats/streams. No native equivalent; would require hand-rolling, which is a legit reason to keep a small focused dep.

deps.name: cors
deps.reason: Configurable CORS middleware (origin/preflight/credentials logic). No Node built-in; correctness-sensitive, keep it.

deps.name: express
deps.reason: Core framework, no native equivalent.

deps.name: node-fetch
deps.reason: Native global fetch only became stable (non-experimental) in Node 21. At a >=18 floor fetch is experimental, so node-fetch is legit at THIS floor. Would flip to needless at >=22.

deps.name: dotenv
deps.reason: Native .env loading (--env-file / process.loadEnvFile) only reached stable in 22.21/24.10 and was experimental before. At >=18 there is no stable native, so dotenv is legit. (Even at >=22 the stable boundary is mid-major, so conservatively legit.)

deps.name: mkdirp
deps.reason: Recursive mkdir via the { recursive: true } option has been stable since Node 10.12; fully replaces mkdirp.

deps.name: zod
deps.reason: Schema validation / parsing with type inference. No native equivalent; validation is exactly the kind of correctness/security work to keep a real lib for.

deps.name: helmet
deps.reason: Security headers middleware (CSP, HSTS, etc.). No Node built-in; security primitive, keep.

deps.name: pino
deps.reason: Structured high-perf JSON logger with levels/serializers/transports. console.log is not an equivalent for production logging. Legit.

deps.name: bcryptjs
deps.reason: Password hashing (bcrypt). node:crypto offers scrypt but bcrypt is a distinct, widely-required algorithm; security primitive. Legit.

deps.name: lodash
deps.reason: Multi-purpose utility belt (dozens of functions). No single native covers it; not replaceable by one built-in. Legit (note: a single lodash.X submodule could be replaceable, but full lodash is not).

deps.reason: v4 ids for fixtures; crypto.randomUUID() stable since Node 16, below the >=18 floor. (v4 only.)

deps.name: chalk
deps.reason: Colorizes custom test reporter/CLI output. Node has util.styleText (experimental in 20, stabilized later) but it is not the stable equivalent at a >=18 floor, so chalk is legit here.

deps.name: jest
deps.reason: Unit framework with mocks/snapshots/coverage. node:test is stable at Node 20 but does not match Jest's snapshot+mock+coverage feature set; Jest is a real multi-purpose choice, not a native-replaceable single util.

deps.name: playwright
deps.reason: Browser automation for e2e; no native equivalent.

deps.name: supertest
deps.reason: Boots an app on an ephemeral port and asserts on HTTP responses fluently for integration tests. fetch+assert is lower-level and lacks supertest's server-binding/teardown and chained expectations. Legit.

deps.name: rimraf
deps.reason: Clears test output; native recursive removal since Node 14.14.

deps.name: rfdc
deps.reason: rfdc ('really fast deep clone') is used to deep-copy fixture/state objects between tests. structuredClone is a global since Node 17, below the >=20 floor, and covers the structured-cloneable data typical of test fixtures. Caveat: structuredClone cannot clone functions/DOM-with-listeners — but plain fixture data is cloneable, so native covers the real use here.

deps.name: dotenv
deps.reason: .env loader. --env-file is experimental in the 20.x line and lacks expansion, so dotenv is legit at >=20.

deps.name: cross-env
deps.reason: Portable inline env-var setting in npm scripts across shells; no Node-runtime native replacement.

cases.stack: Legacy Express REST API on Node 16, mid-modernization: still on old utility deps while the team migrates

deps.name: express
deps.reason: Full HTTP framework: routing, middleware chain, body parsing wiring. No native equivalent; node:http is far lower level.

deps.name: uuid
deps.reason: crypto.randomUUID() is stable since Node 14.17 and covers the v4 use that virtually all projects rely on. Edge case: only v4 — if they used v1/v3/v5 it'd be legit, but a REST API generating request/entity IDs uses v4.

deps.name: node-fetch
deps.reason: Global fetch is experimental at Node 16 (not present until 17.5 behind a flag). At this >=16 floor there is no stable native fetch, so node-fetch is genuinely needed.

deps.name: rimraf
deps.reason: fs.rm/rmSync with recursive+force is stable since Node 14.14 and fully covers recursive directory deletion in a service. rimraf's extra glob support is rarely the real use here.

deps.name: mkdirp
deps.reason: Recursive mkdir is stable since Node 10.12. The whole point of mkdirp (create nested dirs) is a one-flag native call.

deps.name: dotenv
deps.reason: Native --env-file/process.loadEnvFile does not exist before Node 20.6 and is experimental even then. At a >=16 floor there is no native option, so dotenv is required.

deps.name: lodash
deps.reason: Multi-purpose utility belt (debounce, groupBy, deep merge, cloneDeep, etc.). No single native replaces the whole surface; not reducible to one built-in.

deps.name: jsonwebtoken
deps.reason: Security primitive: JWT signing/verification with algorithm handling and claim validation. node:crypto provides HMAC/sign but not the JWT format/verification logic.

cases.stack: Legacy worker/queue service on Node 16 with hand-rolled helpers being phased out

deps.name: left-pad
deps.reason: padStart is ES2017, available since Node 8. Left-padding a string is exactly padStart — zero reason to ship a package.

deps.name: is-odd
deps.reason: Parity is a single arithmetic expression; the language operator fully covers it. (is-odd also pulls is-number — pure bloat.)

deps.name: object-assign
deps.reason: Object.assign is ES2015, native since Node 4. This package is a ponyfill for environments that no longer exist at this floor.

deps.name: abort-controller
deps.reason: Global AbortController is available since Node 15 (stable from 15.4), which is at/below this >=16 floor. The ponyfill is redundant.

deps.name: ioredis
deps.reason: Redis client with connection pooling, cluster/sentinel support, command pipelining. No native Redis protocol support in Node.

deps.name: p-limit
deps.reason: Concurrency limiter for promise pools. No native primitive bounds parallel async tasks; you'd hand-roll a queue otherwise.

deps.name: pino
deps.reason: Structured high-throughput JSON logger with levels, redaction, transports. console.log is not a substitute for production logging.

deps.name: bull
deps.reason: Redis-backed job queue with retries, scheduling, rate limiting. No native job-queue equivalent.

cases.stack: Legacy CLI/data-import tool on Node 18, being modernized off old arg/clone deps

deps.name: minimist
deps.reason: util.parseArgs covers minimist's basic flag/option parsing. Edge case: parseArgs only became stable in Node 20 — at this >=18 floor it is still experimental (18.3+). Borderline, but for a tool that can pin to 18.3+ and accept experimental status the native exists; marking needless because the typical minimist use (booleans/strings/aliases) is fully covered. Conservative reviewers could call this legit until >=20.

deps.name: lodash.clonedeep
deps.reason: structuredClone is a stable global since Node 17, below this >=18 floor, and deep-clones plain data/Map/Set/Date/typed arrays. Single-purpose package fully replaced. Caveat: structuredClone can't clone functions — but data import payloads are plain JSON-like, so it holds.

deps.name: rfdc
deps.reason: rfdc ('really fast deep clone') is a single-purpose deep-clone lib; structuredClone (stable Node 17) replaces it at this floor for cloneable data. Speed differs but the function is covered natively.

deps.name: commander
deps.reason: Multi-purpose CLI framework: subcommands, auto-generated help/usage, option coercion, variadic args. util.parseArgs does not generate help or handle subcommands — not a true replacement.

deps.name: csv-parse
deps.reason: RFC-4180 CSV parsing with quoting/escaping/streaming. No native CSV parser in Node.

deps.name: chalk
deps.reason: Terminal styling with auto color-level detection and TTY/CI awareness. util.styleText (Node 20+/experimental) is above this floor and less capable, so chalk is legit at >=18.

deps.name: ora
deps.reason: Terminal spinner with frames, color, and TTY handling. No native spinner; not a one-liner replacement.

cases.stack: Legacy Express API on Node 18 being hardened; mixed needless and essential security deps

deps.name: node-fetch
deps.reason: Global fetch exists at Node 18 but is marked EXPERIMENTAL until Node 21. At a strict >=18 floor a conservative service legitimately keeps node-fetch rather than depend on an experimental global. (Becomes needless at a >=22 floor.)

deps.name: uuid
deps.reason: crypto.randomUUID() (stable 14.17) covers v4 generation. Standard for request/correlation IDs. Edge case noted: only the v4 path is covered.

deps.name: bcryptjs
deps.reason: Password hashing (bcrypt). node:crypto offers scrypt but not bcrypt; swapping algorithms is a deliberate security decision, not a needless-native case. Security primitive — keep.

deps.name: helmet
deps.reason: Curated set of security HTTP headers (CSP, HSTS, etc.) with sane defaults. No native middleware; rolling your own headers is exactly what helmet exists to prevent.

deps.name: cors
deps.reason: CORS preflight/origin handling middleware for Express. No native CORS handling in node:http.

deps.name: joi
deps.reason: Schema validation with rich constraints and error messages. No native schema validator. (Could be swapped for zod, but that's a lateral move, not a native replacement.)

deps.name: qs
deps.reason: Nested/array query-string parsing (a[b]=c) which URLSearchParams does NOT support. Native URLSearchParams only handles flat pairs, so qs is legit when nested objects are needed.

cases.stack: Legacy GraphQL/Apollo service on Node 18 trimming utility cruft during upgrade

deps.name: clone-deep
deps.reason: Single-purpose deep clone; structuredClone (stable Node 17, below floor) replaces it for cloneable data. Caveat: no function cloning, but resolver context payloads are data.

deps.name: is-number
deps.reason: Numeric type check is a native one-liner (Number.isFinite / typeof). Micro-package with no justification.

deps.name: querystringify
deps.reason: Flat query-string encode/decode is exactly URLSearchParams (global since Node 10). Replaceable as long as nested objects aren't needed — which is querystringify's flat-only scope.

deps.name: graphql
deps.reason: Reference GraphQL implementation: parsing, validation, execution engine. Obviously no native equivalent.

deps.name: @apollo/server
deps.reason: GraphQL server: schema wiring, plugins, request lifecycle. No native equivalent.

deps.name: dataloader
deps.reason: Batching/caching layer to solve N+1 in resolvers. No native primitive does request-scoped batching.

deps.name: graphql-tag
deps.reason: Parses GraphQL SDL into AST documents (gql template tag). Tied to graphql; no native parser.

deps.name: pino
deps.reason: Structured production logger; console is not a substitute. No native equivalent.

cases.stack: Legacy file-processing service on Node 20 with fs/clone helpers left over from older Node

deps.name: rimraf
deps.reason: Recursive force delete is native since 14.14, well below this >=20 floor. rimraf's only remaining edge (glob deletion) is rarely the actual use.

deps.name: mkdirp
deps.reason: Recursive mkdir native since 10.12. Fully redundant at >=20.

deps.name: fast-copy
deps.reason: Single-purpose deep clone; structuredClone stable since 17, below floor. Replaced for structured-cloneable data (the file-metadata objects here qualify).

deps.name: minimist
deps.reason: util.parseArgs became STABLE in Node 20, at this floor. It covers minimist's flag/string/boolean parsing for a processing script's CLI. Now a clean native replacement (unlike at the >=18 case).

deps.name: sharp
deps.reason: High-performance image processing (libvips bindings): resize, format conversion. No native image manipulation in Node.

deps.name: archiver
deps.reason: Streaming ZIP/TAR creation with entries from streams/files. node:zlib only does gzip/deflate streams, not archive containers, so archiver is legit.

deps.name: mime-types
deps.reason: Maps extensions to MIME types via a maintained database. No native MIME lookup table in Node.

deps.name: p-map
deps.reason: Concurrency-bounded async map over an iterable. No native bounded-concurrency map; Promise.all is unbounded.

cases.stack: Legacy Koa API on Node 20 shedding promise/clone micro-deps

deps.name: node-fetch
deps.reason: Global fetch is present at Node 20 but still marked experimental until Node 21. A strict >=20 floor sits just under the stable line, so a cautious team may keep node-fetch. Becomes clearly needless at >=22.

deps.name: uuid
deps.reason: v4 UUID generation is native (14.17). Standard correlation-ID use is covered. Only-v4 caveat applies.

deps.name: rfdc
deps.reason: Single-purpose fast deep clone; structuredClone (stable 17) replaces it at this floor for cloneable data.

deps.name: p-defer
deps.reason: p-defer returns {promise, resolve, reject}. Promise.withResolvers() does exactly that — BUT it's only available from Node 22, ABOVE this >=20 floor. So at >=20 there is NO native and p-defer is actually LEGIT. Wait — correcting: at this floor native is unavailable, label must be legit.

deps.name: koa
deps.reason: HTTP framework: middleware cascade, context object, async flow. No native equivalent.

deps.name: koa-router
deps.reason: Routing layer for Koa (params, methods, nesting). No native router.

deps.name: zod
deps.reason: TypeScript-first schema validation with inference. No native runtime validator.

deps.name: axios
deps.reason: HTTP client used here for interceptors, automatic transforms, and instance config — features fetch lacks. Multi-purpose, not a thin fetch wrapper in this use.

cases.stack: Legacy build/tooling package on Node 20 cleaning out fs and string shims

deps.name: glob
deps.reason: Full glob matching with brace/extglob/ignore semantics. fs.glob exists only from Node 22 (and was experimental), ABOVE this >=20 floor, so glob is legit at >=20.

deps.name: rimraf
deps.reason: Recursive force delete native since 14.14. Redundant at >=20.

deps.name: make-dir
deps.reason: make-dir is mkdirp's successor; recursive mkdir is native since 10.12. Fully covered.

deps.name: cpy / cp wrapper (cpr)
deps.reason: Recursive copy is native via fs.cp/cpSync since Node 16.7 (stable from ~18). At >=20 the recursive-copy package is redundant for plain dir copies.

deps.name: chokidar
deps.reason: Robust cross-platform file watching that normalizes fs.watch's well-known inconsistencies (recursive, dedup, atomic-save handling). Native fs.watch is unreliable, so chokidar stays legit.

deps.name: esbuild
deps.reason: Bundler/transpiler. No native bundler in Node.

deps.name: semver
deps.reason: Semantic-version parsing/range satisfaction. No native semver logic.

deps.name: picocolors
deps.reason: Terminal coloring; util.styleText is experimental at this floor and less ergonomic, so picocolors is legit at >=20.

cases.stack: Modernized Fastify service finally on Node 22; legacy http/clone/env deps now removable

deps.name: node-fetch
deps.reason: Global fetch became STABLE in Node 21, below this >=22 floor. node-fetch is now fully redundant for standard requests.

deps.name: uuid
deps.reason: v4 generation native since 14.17. Correlation/entity IDs covered. Only-v4 caveat.

deps.name: lodash.clonedeep
deps.reason: Deep clone of cloneable data is native (structuredClone, stable 17). Single-purpose package fully replaced.

deps.name: abort-controller
deps.reason: Global AbortController stable since 15.4, far below floor. Ponyfill redundant.

deps.name: dotenv
deps.reason: Native --env-file/process.loadEnvFile exists but stabilized only in 22.21 and lacks ${VAR} expansion and .env cascade. Conservative call: keep dotenv legit at >=22 since the native lacks expansion most projects rely on. Borderline — a project not using expansion could drop it.

deps.name: fastify
deps.reason: High-performance HTTP framework with schema-based validation/serialization and plugin system. No native equivalent.

deps.name: pino
deps.reason: Structured logger (Fastify's default). console is not a substitute.

deps.name: jsonwebtoken
deps.reason: JWT sign/verify security primitive. node:crypto lacks JWT format/claim handling.

cases.stack: Modernized internal API on Node 22 where most legacy shims are now native

deps.name: rimraf
deps.reason: Recursive force delete native since 14.14. Redundant at >=22.

deps.name: mkdirp
deps.reason: Recursive mkdir native since 10.12. Redundant.

deps.name: minimist
deps.reason: util.parseArgs stable since Node 20, below floor. Covers minimist's flag parsing for internal scripts.

deps.name: p-defer
deps.reason: Promise.withResolvers() became available/stable in Node 22, AT this floor, and returns the exact {promise, resolve, reject} deferred shape p-defer provides. Now natively replaceable.

deps.name: rfdc
deps.reason: Single-purpose deep clone replaced by structuredClone (stable 17) for cloneable data.

deps.name: express
deps.reason: HTTP framework: routing/middleware. No native equivalent.

deps.name: ws
deps.reason: WebSocket server/client. Node's experimental WebSocket global is client-only; ws is needed for a SERVER, so it's legit even at >=22.

deps.name: pg
deps.reason: PostgreSQL client/driver (wire protocol, pooling). No native Postgres support; node:sqlite doesn't apply.

cases.stack: Legacy monolith on Node 16 with assorted polyfills accumulated over years

deps.name: es6-promise
deps.reason: Native Promises since Node 0.12/4. A Promise polyfill at a >=16 floor is pure dead weight.

deps.name: core-js
deps.reason: Standard-library polyfill suite, normally pulled transitively by a transpiler's targets config. Whether needed depends on browser/old-runtime targets, not Node version — not a clean single-native swap, so legit by default to avoid a wrong needless call.

deps.name: node-uuid
deps.reason: node-uuid (the deprecated predecessor of uuid) for v4 IDs is replaced by crypto.randomUUID() (14.17), below this floor. Only-v4 caveat.

deps.name: deep-equal
deps.reason: util.isDeepStrictEqual (Node 9) covers deep structural equality, the core use of deep-equal. Edge: deep-equal's loose (==) mode differs, but strict deep compare is the typical intent.

deps.name: moment
deps.reason: Date parsing/formatting/manipulation/timezone surface. Intl + Date cover parts but not moment's whole API; it's multi-purpose (and a lateral swap to a lighter lib, not a native removal).

deps.name: request
deps.reason: Deprecated but multi-feature HTTP client (streaming, cookies, proxies, multipart). At a >=16 floor there's no stable native fetch; removing it is a migration, not a one-line native swap, so legit at this floor.

deps.name: winston
deps.reason: Logger with transports/levels/formats. No native structured logging.
